examples: The little tadpole, about the size of a sesame seed, is now made up of three layers: the ectoderm, mesoderm and endoderm.
The little tadpole about the size of a sesame seed is now made up of three layers: the ectoderm mesoderm and endoderm.
At this stage the structure, though still just a featureless ball of cells, has started to differentiate into the body's three basic cell types (known as endoderm, mesoderm and ectoderm).
